related_quote: I have made numerous suggestions (as the Scrum Master), I think four times, in the past year but it fell on deaf ears.

related_quote: How can I deal with a developer who isn't on board with a new PM and programming framework? [...] but I just can't seem to get this person on board by committing to our Sprint goals and work tools (i.e. Rails).

challenge_identified: Product ownership responsibilities are unclear and unaddressed.
related_quote: Organization fails to deliver a Product Owner [...]. However, not having a product owner does slow us down and demoralizes the team in my opinion, because nobody seems to want to take responsibility for the final product except

challenge_identified: Manager's inconsistent responses disrupt the focus of the meeting.
related_quote: Sometimes he replies to the SM 'hold on, we need to discuss this', therefore the SM has given up trying to refocus the discussion.

challenge_identified: Fear of being perceived as a project dictator during 'crunch time', leading to frustration and annoyance.
related_quote: Balance between sharing creative control and leading the project. [...] I don't want to be a project dictator but when we are in "crunch time" and somebody goes off on a tangent with an idea and reports back that he doesn't think my solution is ideal, I find myself becoming aggravated.

challenge_identified: Silence during questions leads to lack of diversity and inefficiency.
related_quote: How to encourage more participation in web scrum meetings? [...] I often times have very long periods of silence after I ask a question. [...] While I don't worry too much about silence, from individual discussions I know there are things that are not being brought up, and I also feel we are not being very efficient where the meeting is longer than it should be due to the silence.

challenge_identified: The team struggles with maintaining focus and productivity during project meetings.
related_quote: A development workflow for a team of 3 developers that minimizes outside interruptions? [...] but where my team fails is when we are all working on separate projects and we get bombarded with input from many other departments.

challenge_identified: Inadequate soft skills training and lack of emphasis on communication skills among team members lead to difficulties in effectively conveying customer requirements and building client trust.
related_quote: Incorrect pronunciation leads to miscommunication which impacts customer confidence [...] problems for the team in understanding customer requirements [...] Team not successful in convincing clients when requirement creeps [...] Soft skills trainings are not usually conducted in small companies [...] Team do not give importance to communication skills and they believe it is not a part of their job

challenge_identified: Concerns about the complexity of stories leading to disagreements during estimation.
related_quote: How should a Scrum master handle disagreement about story-point estimates in Scrum? [...] I understand your opinion, but don't think those complications are valid. [...] The 20-point voters say, 'The past tells us that these things are always a lot more complicated than they seem.'

challenge_identified: She feels threatened by taking personal responsibility for decisions and may resist change.
related_quote: For some reason (possibly culturally as she's from another country) she takes any decisions made by the PM's personally though will back down when her manager asks her to do something [...] If I ask her to refactor some code to make it easier to manage, she will start into a tirade with any number of excuses as to why it needs to stay the way she wrote it. [...] If her manager asks her to make a similar change she does so without question. [...] This is why I wondered if it were a cultural issue because clearly, she respects only the authority which can fire her

challenge_identified: Defensive behavior towards reporting needs, questioning reports, and resisting perceived necessity.
related_quote: Driving Changes on a peer-to-peer level [...] If I would be the leader, I would just say. "Stop it. Don't waste time for this report" [... but I am not the leader. I know, that if you ask "Why do you need this report?", then the others will find a lot of reasons why they think the report is needed. [... The more I ask into the direction like "stop it", the more the others will think they need to defend what they did in the past.

challenge_identified: Resistance to change and lack of initiative among team members.
related_quote: Every time I walk into their sprint planning session, I do not like what I see. [...] It's absolute silence and we can practically hear ourselves breathing. [...] The SM is the only person talking and tries to create tasks, one-by-one, for each story. [...] I try to encourage the PO and developers to ask questions, but I have developers who are very "passive" (not pro-active). [...] No developer will voluntarily request to be assigned a task (like I used to do and have seen done by so many of my peers). [...] They sit and almost wait to be assigned tasks (which was how it was before I introduced Agile).

challenge_identified: Front-End team produces lot of bugs (defects) inside a sprint cycle.
related_quote: How to manage a failed sub team inside a scrum team. [...] We are in a loop of pointing out the issues -> Team records it -> on next sprint, same issues -> Retrospective [...] Front-End team produces lot of bugs (defects) inside a sprint cycle. [...] Even they spend overtime, all nights, double shifts, they only can resolve 90% of the bugs within a given sprint period. [...] The remaining 10% is mostly the complex bugs. [...] Even we provide few extra days or in some cases an extra week, they fail to deliver.

challenge_identified: Agile meetings exceeding 15 minutes frequently, with Scrum master unsure of intervention responsibilities.
related_quote: This results in the Daily Scrum meeting going beyond 15-min. almost daily. [...] My Query: Is it the responsibility of the Scrum master to intervene and interrupt a team member when he/she starts talking about points beyond those 3 that I mentioned? [...] Is this OK as per Agile?

challenge_identified: They are taking longer and are less effective now we are working from home because of COVID
related_quote: They are taking longer and are less effective now we are working from home because of COVID

challenge_identified: Customer delays causing urgent fixes

related_quote: Software development team does NOT report to PM, but PM is responsible for hitting milestone dates and not exceeding budget

[...] This becomes a nightmare as trying to manage the client becomes almost impossible. [...] Now the logical thing to say here is that we are over committing each sprint or underestimating stories which is very possible.

challenge_identified: I struggle with expressing my doubts and concerns regarding the feasibility of new projects.
related_quote: When presented with a new project, which I can rather quickly dismiss as unfeasible (we're talking extremely unrealistic projects), I have trouble communicating why it cannot be done. [...] I argue the technical complexity and the requirements versus the time and price scope.

challenge_identified: Concerns about the importance of video conferencing during ceremonies.
related_quote: How to influence a scrum team to do something you as a SM feel is beneficial to the team. [...] Given that the team cannot change its structure in the short term, having developers actively participating on testing meant a great advantage for the whole team and we were actually being able to have shippable versions at the end of each sprint.

challenge_identified: I feel like my ideas are ignored during meetings.
related_quote: Whenever I try to voice my ideas/opinions during meetings they are abandoned/ignored. [...]

challenge_identified: Fear of speaking up and lack of engagement from team members.

[...] When I asked him not to speak first (he is willing to change this state) there was silence and it was really hard to end our meetings with any conclusions. [...] The guys are simply fine with everything

challenge_identified: It's such a confusing prospect for me as a scrum master because since they deliver at pace, they have not vocalized any issues, stakeholders are happy there appears to be no problem but on the other hand it's not really what I would call a scrum team.

challenge_identified: I feel like I would close down and not give my actual feedback.

[...] my gut feelings say "standards are usually good", which leads me to prefer view A. [...] But as I said, others on my team disagree

challenge_identified: jam sessions

[...] Often the PO has asked the customer, or knows already, but the product UI ends up being quite cluttered and complex.

challenge_identified: I'm experiencing persisting pain in the back, and it's medically proved that it's present and that it will never improve but only degrade. related_quote: I seem to be repeatedly stuck in a situation where release dates are set not based on anything technical, but because someone in Sales has committed to a customer by then

related_quote: I am starting to regret not taking any business classes in college because I don't understand the first thing about running a business so I don't always understand what people are wanting, the best I can do is 'think through it'.

related_quote: I feel like once I am stuck on a problem, I find myself closing up, unable to voice it, as if asking for help is a sign of incompetency.

challenge_identified: Concerns about potential loss of work due to a possible bug causing project abandonment.
related_quote: So, what kind of argument should I make to my boss? [...] Or is he right, and there could be a danger of losing all our work from some weird bug?

challenge_identified: Concerns regarding lack of involvement in design decisions and perceived unfairness among team members.
related_quote: What about a member of the team working above and beyond the expectations put forth during planning and execution of sprints? [...] Other developers complain about not being involved in design decisions related to these stories, since the work was done out of band.

challenge_identified: Rules should be more flexible and allow for exceptions.
related_quote: There are several rules I must keep asking programmers to follow very often. [...] They write code and, if it works, job is just done, for them.

challenge_identified: I end up spending a lot of my day just checking what they did, and fixing bugs and cases they didn't think of.

challenge_identified: Constant requests for working code interrupt thoughts and concentration.

challenge_identified: Resistance to change and learning anxiety
related_quote: But still, they are sometimes reluctant to accept my suggestion, partly because they have invested so much in their own algorithm, or partly because of the fear that using a new method would entail more learning time and make them appear to the management as if they are going nowhere.

challenge_identified: Project manager gets upset when tasks spend multiple days in development.

challenge_identified: How do we effectively implement new development standards and improve our project processes while minimizing developer resistance?

challenge_identified: I've only been on this team for a week, but I see so many issues with their codebase that I imagine I'll be spending more time fighting with what's already written to do things in 'their way' than I would if I were working on a project that, for example, followed more widely accepted coding standards, architecture patterns, and best practices.
related_quote: I've only been on this team for a week, but I see so many issues with their codebase that I imagine I'll be spending more time fighting with what's already written to do things in 'their way' than I would if I were working on a project that, for example, followed more widely accepted coding standards, architecture patterns, and best practices.

challenge_identified: I find myself going over and over the same issues with them.

Should you verbalize your disagreement, or just not and listen to them?

challenge_identified: Persistent problems with completing planned work in sprints, high carryover, lack of learning from past mistakes.
related_quote: I've been here for a year and we have a very persistent problem: almost everybody can't finish planned work in almost every sprint. We have huge carryovers in every sprint. [...] I tend to vote for larger estimates, but my teammates almost never learn from their past mistakes and persistently vote low in these estimations.

challenge_identified: Long discussions inhibit innovation and prevent making quick decisions.
related_quote: In a software team, how do you cut through these long discussions without inhibiting innovation, so that a decision can be made and you can get on to solving the real business problems.

challenge_identified: Concerns regarding soliciting product suggestions and feedback from clients.
related_quote: Our initial thought was to solicit product suggestions and feedback on mock-ups from certain members of our current clientele who have made multiple feature suggestions in the past. This seemed good in theory,

challenge_identified: Lack of effective communication among team members leading to misunderstandings and delays.
related_quote: Recently I've come to understand that a serious problem at my workplace is the lack of communication between developers. [...] Whenever I create some component or a naming convention or whatever... there's no way I can make it known to all the people who might need to know this. [...] At most I tell it in person to the fellow who asked me for it, but that's about it.

challenge_identified: Code smells and bad engineering practices are a real problem.
related_quote: When I look at our code, I see some code smells and bad engineering practices [...] The latter two seem to be a real problem. [...] I want to convince my teammates to use smaller classes and methods. [...] But should I do that? If yes, then how?

challenge_identified: Should I not care if the code is littered with redundant identifiers and not descriptive names, and continue to use my own way and push for that to become the standard? [...] I've brought up standardizing on the .Net naming conventions in the past and it's just ignored
related_quote: Should I not care if the code is littered with redundant identifiers and not descriptive names, and continue to use my own way and push for that to become the standard? [...] I've brought up standardizing on the .Net naming conventions in the past and it's just ignored

challenge_identified: I feel like my contributions aren't recognized or appreciated within the company.
related_quote: But I haven't found the company to give any sort of encouragement / appreciation. [...] My seniors who were not involved in the project were given praise, leave, bonuses, etc. [...] Also I was rejected permission to attend an important family function - that makes me ask 'what's the credit I have now'.

challenge_identified: We're getting flak from our support staff and marketing department that they don't feel that they're getting enough lead time on new features and notifications on bug fixes that could change behavior.
related_quote: However - we're getting flak from our support staff and marketing department that they don't feel that they're getting enough lead time on new features and notifications on bug fixes that could change behavior.

challenge_identified: Whether everything in this list should be recorded as a 'defect' - some of the issues they found would be better described as refinements, or even 'nice-to-haves', and some we think are not defects at all.
related_quote: We don't want to come across as being difficult, or taking things too personally here, and we are happy to make all of the changes identified, however we are a bit concerned especially as there is an uneven power balance at play in our relationship. [...] They client's QA lead says that it is standard for them to label every issue they identify as a defect, however, we are a bit uncomfortable about this. Whilst the relationship is good, we don't see a huge problem with this, but we are concerned that, if the relationship suffers in the future, these lists of 'defects' could prove costly for us.

challenge_identified: Design choices may not always align with user needs.
related_quote: How to explain why design choices are good?

challenge_identified: Identifying whether a feature is a bug, task, or extra feature
related_quote: This is where the problems appear. [...] Some say that it is a bug others say it is a task or an extra feature. [...] here start the discussions [...] is this a bug or an extra feature / task?

challenge_identified: Concerns about documentation requirements during retrospectives, suggesting potential inefficiencies and lack of immediate action.
related_quote: Our Scrum master wants us to document our feedback for the iteration before our Retrospective. [...] Her argument is that she wants us to have time to document our feedback. [...] Another guy on the thinks that we should be documenting our feedback during the meeting or right before the meeting.

challenge_identified: I feel so sad and hurt. I really love programming and making them say something like that really hurts me.
related_quote: For the first time, someone reviews my code and he says it is a mess. [...] I feel so sad and hurt. I really love programming and making them say something like that really hurts me.

challenge_identified: Perception of poor performance leading to emotional distress and potential conflict.
related_quote: How to tell whether your programmers are under-performing? [...] I am a team lead with 5+ developers. [...] I've a straight talk with him regarding my perception on his performance. [...] He was indignant when I suggested that I had the feeling that he wasn't performing at his best level. [...] He felt that this was a completely unfair feeling.

challenge_identified: When and how (if ever) should we express the difference in opinions?
related_quote: Our project head is a genius software architect, a gentle and considerate person in general, a geek by nature and delicate by voice. [...] But, at times, we (my teammates and I) differ in opinions -- especially of software architecture issues, system design issues, UI issues, etc., with our leader. [...] When and how (if ever) should we express the difference in opinions?

challenge_identified: Concerns regarding the balance between developer/client interaction and overall company culture.
related_quote: How much should Developers interact with Clients? [...] My manager, and old-school mainframe guy, contends that developers should never ever have ANY client contact. [...] His boss, the owner of the company, told him this morning that every single person in the company needs to think of themselves as an extension of the sales department, and needs to be listening all the time for upsell opportunities.

challenge_identified: Fear of shortening deadlines due to CTO's concern, leading to increased stress on programmers.
related_quote: To do this I have discussion with related team players and calculate a deadline estimation. [...] Sometimes, I got a scared voice from CTO saying that the estimated deadline is too much. [...] Then I have to shorten the deadline. [...] With a shorter deadline, programmers have to work with extra pressure.

challenge_identified: Code review conflicts leading to premature changes and potential bugs.
related_quote: However, sometimes he reviews my code (he's second in command to our team leader, so that's expected) without a heads up. [...] So sometimes he reviews my changes before they complete the end goal and makes changes right away [...] and has even broken my work once.

challenge_identified: The software development process has not been updated for years, leading to potential product and team failure.
related_quote: I am interested in knowing how to deal with a current software development process that has not been changed for years and will eventually lead to product and team failure.

challenge_identified: I feel like I'm explaining myself too much, and my coworkers aren't getting it.
related_quote: However, I have good reason to believe my explanations are not effective. I routinely have hour long discussions trying to explain my code, and on many occasions the conversations have ended with my coworker saying they still don't understand, but they have somewhere else to be (lunch, or home, or a meeting, etc.).

challenge_identified: Product Owner frequently adding new stories to the Product Backlog without considering existing backlog items.
related_quote: For the last 3 sprints or so, I've observed our Product Owner suddenly shoving new stories - stories not previously in the Product Backlog -- at the top of the Product Backlog. [...] My question is - how much can the Product Owner really shake up the Product Backlog? [...] Is it really fair game to ignore what's already in the backlog and keep on shoving new stories at the top?

challenge_identified: Feeling overwhelmed and unsure how to address the situation involving the new developer.
related_quote: So now I want to know what to do. I've spent a lot of my time on this project, so I don't want to just get up and leave, but I'm finding it hard to work with this new developer. [...] On the flip side, he is now the #1 committer on the project, with even more commits than the lead developer. [...] I'm not really sure what to do about this. [...] Has anybody else experienced this problem? If so, what did you do?

challenge_identified: Pushback from developer regarding acceptance criteria and edge cases
related_quote: I am getting push back from one of the developers when I reject his stories. [...] He says it's unfair since I don't specify the edge cases and how the program should respond in the acceptance criteria, as he tends to code for only what I describe in the story.
